summary: A car‑ramming attack at Gush Etzion Junction injured multiple civilians and prompted emergency medical response. Hezbollah launched numerous rocket and missile attacks on Israeli positions in southern Lebanon, while the IDF reported hostile aircraft intrusions over northern Israel and continued airstrikes on Hezbollah sites in Tyre. Iran announced the passage of dozens of commercial vessels through the Strait of Hormuz under its navy’s protection and its negotiators reiterated that no US‑Iran agreement will be accepted without securing Iranian rights.
key_points: Car‑ramming attack at Gush Etzion Junction injured at least four civilians, including two in serious condition, and the attacker was neutralised (Source: @yediotnews25/130213).

Hezbollah reported firing guided missiles at an Israeli Merkava tank and multiple rocket attacks on Israeli forces in the village of al‑Biyadah and other locations in southern Lebanon (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/364302).
The IDF detected hostile aircraft intrusions over northern Israel, including near Rosh HaNikra, Betzet Beach and Shlomi, and launched interceptions (Source: @tzevaadom_en/67882).
Israeli air force continued strikes on Hezbollah targets in Tyre and southern Lebanon, publishing footage of a strike on a Hezbollah command centre (Source: @manniefabian/58336).
Iran’s IRGC‑affiliated navy reported escorting 28 commercial vessels, including oil tankers and container ships, through the Strait of Hormuz (Source: @VahidOnline/75814).
Iranian top negotiator Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf stated no agreement with the United States will be accepted until Iran’s rights are secured (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3203).
Jordan condemned an incursion by Israeli settlers into the al‑Aqsa Mosque compound, calling it provocative and reckless (Source: @presstv/192157).

sections.executive_summary: A car‑ramming attack at Gush Etzion Junction injured multiple civilians and led to emergency medical treatment (Source: @yediotnews25/130213). Hezbollah launched a series of missile and rocket attacks on Israeli positions across southern Lebanon, prompting IDF airstrikes on Hezbollah sites in Tyre (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/364302; @manniefabian/58336). Iran announced the escorted passage of 28 commercial vessels through the Strait of Hormuz and its negotiators reiterated that no US‑Iran deal will be accepted without securing Iranian rights (Source: @VahidOnline/75814; @Faytuks_Network/3203).

points: Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf declared that no agreement with the United States will be approved until Tehran is certain that the rights of the Iranian people have been secured, emphasizing a hardline negotiating stance (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3203).

The IRGC‑affiliated naval forces reported that 28 commercial vessels, including oil tankers and container ships, passed through the Strait of Hormuz under Iranian naval escort and security coordination (Source: @VahidOnline/75814).
An explosion sound was reported near Qeshm Island, Iran, with residents confirming hearing it but the nature of the event remained unknown (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3209).
Iranian media denied the resignation of a senior medical official, indicating internal political stability within the IRGC‑linked institutions (Source: @shin_persian/6332).

The IDF reported multiple hostile aircraft intrusions over northern Israel, with identified confrontation lines at Rosh HaNikra, Betzet Beach, Milouot Industrial Zone, Shlomi and Hanita, and launched interceptions to neutralise the threats (Source: @tzevaadom_en/67882).

Israeli forces responded to a car‑ramming attack at Gush Etzion Junction, treating four injured civilians, including two in serious condition, and neutralising the attacker (Source: @yediotnews25/130213; @yediotnews25/130215).
The IDF released footage of strikes on a Hezbollah command centre in the Tyre area and confirmed ongoing air operations against Hezbollah sites in southern Lebanon (Source: @manniefabian/58336).

Medical teams from MDA provided emergency care to victims of the Gush Etzion attack, evacuating a 17‑year‑old girl with severe limb injuries and a 15‑year‑old with facial injuries to local hospitals (Source: @yediotnews25/130217).

A car‑ramming attack at Gush Etzion Junction in the West Bank resulted in multiple injuries, with reports of two serious and two moderate casualties before the attacker was neutralised (Source: @yediotnews25/130213; @almogboker78/17240).
Preliminary reports indicated a suspect vehicle was shot at the scene of the Gush Etzion attack, with medical services confirming injuries (Source: @pkpoi/142328).
No further incidents were reported in Gaza or the broader West Bank within the provided messages.
No specific casualty figures or operational details were provided for Gaza during this time frame.

Hezbollah announced a series of 21 military actions on 30‑31 May, including guided missile strikes on an Israeli Merkava tank in al‑Biyadah, multiple rocket attacks on Israeli forces in villages such as al‑Biyadah, Shlomi, Dibl, and Qozah, and artillery fire on Israeli positions near Hanita and Safed (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/364302).
The IDF captured the Beaufort Castle in southern Lebanon, releasing footage of Golani Brigade troops securing the historic site (Source: @manniefabian/58344).
Jordan condemned an Israeli settlers’ incursion into the al‑Aqsa Mosque compound, reflecting regional diplomatic tensions linked to the Lebanon front (Source: @presstv/192157).
Hezbollah leader Nabaeh Bari pledged to disarm Hezbollah if Israel halted attacks, while simultaneously urging Israel to continue its offensive towards Beirut (Source: @rodast_omiddana/72226).
No direct operational reports concerning Syria or Iraq were present in the supplied messages.
Iranian statements and naval movements through the Strait of Hormuz may have indirect implications for regional security dynamics involving Iraq and Syria.
No specific incidents, attacks, or military movements were cited for Syria or Iraq.

Iran’s IRGC‑affiliated navy escorted 28 commercial vessels through the Strait of Hormuz, indicating a significant maritime security operation affecting Gulf shipping lanes (Source: @VahidOnline/75814).
U.S. officials expressed concern over potential financial gains for Iran under a nuclear agreement, with former President Trump criticizing media coverage of the deal (Source: @lelotsenzura/92382).
No direct military actions by Gulf states were reported in the provided messages.

key_developments: Hezbollah launched a guided missile strike on an Israeli Merkava tank in al‑Biyadah, confirming a confirmed hit (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/364302).
The IDF reported hostile aircraft intrusions over northern Israel, identifying multiple confrontation lines and conducting interceptions (Source: @tzevaadom_en/67882).
A car‑ramming attack at Gush Etzion Junction injured four civilians, including two in serious condition, and the attacker was neutralised (Source: @yediotnews25/130213).
Iranian negotiator Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf stated no US‑Iran agreement will be accepted without securing Iranian rights (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3203).
Iran’s IRGC‑affiliated navy escorted 28 commercial vessels through the Strait of Hormuz under its protection (Source: @VahidOnline/75814).
The IDF conducted airstrikes on a Hezbollah command centre in the Tyre area and continued strikes on southern Lebanon targets (Source: @manniefabian/58336).
Jordan condemned an Israeli settlers’ incursion into the al‑Aqsa Mosque compound, labeling it provocative and reckless (Source: @presstv/192157).
sections.threat_assessment: Hezbollah’s intensive missile and rocket campaign across southern Lebanon demonstrates a heightened capability to strike Israeli forces and infrastructure, increasing the risk of broader escalation. Israel’s detection of multiple hostile aircraft over its northern border indicates a potential expansion of aerial threats, possibly involving state or proxy actors. Iran’s naval escort of commercial vessels through the Strait of Hormuz underscores its willingness to assert maritime control, which could affect regional shipping security and provoke counter‑measures.
sections.regional_response: Jordan’s diplomatic condemnation of Israeli settler actions at al‑Aqsa reflects growing regional criticism of Israeli operations, potentially influencing broader Arab positions. The United States, while not directly mentioned in operational reports, is implicated through political statements about Iran’s financial gains, suggesting continued diplomatic pressure. Iran’s firm stance on negotiations signals limited flexibility, likely sustaining tension in nuclear talks and affecting regional diplomatic dynamics.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of Hezbollah’s missile launch sites and supply lines is critical to anticipate further attacks on Israeli positions. Continued surveillance of aerial activity over northern Israel is needed to identify the origin of hostile aircraft intrusions. Tracking Iranian naval movements in the Strait of Hormuz will provide insight into Tehran’s strategic maritime objectives and potential flashpoints with Gulf states.
